lastSessionSummary: Initial plan created. Admin portal replaces the old Firebase-based admin dashboard. Will be built after TestFlight launch using React + Vite + Tailwind + Supabase (service_role key). All phases planned and ready to execute.
nextSteps: Complete mobile app TestFlight build first
Begin Phase 0: Admin Scaffold & Auth after TestFlight is live
Set up Supabase admin role and service_role key access
openQuestions: Hosting: Vercel (same as marketing site) or separate deployment?
Admin auth: Supabase email/password with admin role check, or separate auth?
Support email integration: Resend (already used in app) or dedicated support tool?

phases.name: Project Scaffold & Admin Auth
phases.description: New Vite + React + Tailwind project with Supabase admin authentication. Service role key access, admin role verification, and protected route layout.

tasks.name: Create new Vite + React + TypeScript project in rv-neighbors-admin/
Install and configure Tailwind CSS with brand theme
Install Supabase JS client (service_role key for admin operations)
Install React Router for page routing
Install React Query for data fetching/caching
Install Zod for form validation
Create .env with SUPABASE_URL and SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
DB migration: create admin_users table (user_id FK, role, permissions, created_at)
DB migration: RLS policies for admin_users (only service_role can read/write)
Build admin login screen (email/password via Supabase Auth)
Build admin role verification middleware (check admin_users table after login)
Build protected route wrapper (redirect to login if not admin)
Build admin shell layout (sidebar nav, header, main content area)
Configure Vercel deployment with environment variables
Set up .gitignore (node_modules, .env, dist)
Install testing: Vitest + React Testing Library

gateTests.name: Vite dev server starts without errors
Tailwind renders styled components with brand colors
Supabase client connects with service_role key
Admin login → session created, role verified
Non-admin login → rejected with error message
Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to login
Sidebar navigation renders all menu items
.env is gitignored and not committed
Vitest runs with passing placeholder test
Deprecation review: Vite, React 19, Tailwind v4, Supabase JS, React Router v7

phases.name: User Management — Browse & View
phases.description: Browse all user profiles with search/filter/sort. View full user detail including profile, connections, subscription, activity history.

tasks.name: Build Users list page with paginated table (name, email, tier, status, joined date)
Add search bar (search by name, email, or user ID)
Add filter controls (by subscription tier, account status, join date range)
Add sort controls (by name, joined date, last active, subscription tier)
Build User Detail page — Profile section (photo, name, bio, RV info, interests)
Build User Detail page — Account section (email, auth provider, created date, last login)
Build User Detail page — Subscription section (current tier, start date, renewal date, RevenueCat ID)
Build User Detail page — Connections section (count, list of connected users)
Build User Detail page — Family section (family members, role, invite status)
Build User Detail page — Activity section (last check-in, location sharing status, off-the-grid status)
Build User Detail page — Message history section (recent conversations, message count)
Create useAdminUsers hook (list, search, filter, paginate)
Create useAdminUserDetail hook (full profile with related data)
Create reusable DataTable component (pagination, sorting, row click)
Create reusable SearchFilter component

gateTests.name: Users list loads and displays paginated results
Search by name returns matching users
Search by email returns matching users
Filter by subscription tier works (Free, Premium, Family Premium)
Filter by account status works (active, disabled)
Sort by each column works correctly
Pagination navigates between pages correctly
Click user row → navigates to full detail page
User detail shows all profile fields
User detail shows connection count and list
User detail shows subscription info with RevenueCat data
User detail shows family members if applicable
E2E: search user → click → view full detail → navigate back to list
Deprecation review: React Query patterns, Supabase query builders, pagination approach

phases.name: User Management — Actions
phases.description: Disable/enable accounts, trigger password resets, edit user profiles. All actions logged for audit trail.

tasks.name: DB migration: create admin_audit_log table (admin_id, action, target_user_id, details, created_at)
DB migration: RLS policy for admin_audit_log (only service_role can write, admins can read)
Create Edge Function: admin-disable-user (uses Supabase Admin API to ban/unban user)
Create Edge Function: admin-reset-password (sends password reset email via Supabase Auth)
Build Disable Account button with confirmation modal on User Detail page
Build Enable Account button (re-enable disabled accounts)
Build Reset Password button with confirmation on User Detail page
Build Edit User Profile modal (admin can update display name, bio, RV info)
Build Admin Audit Log page (table of all admin actions, filterable by action type and admin)
Add audit log entry on every admin action (disable, enable, reset, edit)
Add action history section to User Detail page (shows admin actions on this user)
Create useAdminActions hook (disable, enable, resetPassword, editProfile)
Create useAuditLog hook (list, filter, paginate)
Build reusable ConfirmationModal component

gateTests.name: Disable account → user cannot log in to mobile app
Enable account → user can log in again
Reset password → user receives password reset email
Edit profile → changes reflected in user's mobile app profile
All actions create audit log entries
Audit log shows admin who performed the action
Confirmation modal prevents accidental actions
Disabled user shows visual indicator in Users list
User Detail page shows action history for that user
Audit Log page filters by action type correctly
E2E: disable user → verify login blocked → re-enable → verify login works
Deprecation review: Supabase Admin API methods, Edge Function Deno patterns

phases.name: Subscription Management
phases.description: View, update, and manage user subscriptions. Issue free upgrades, override tiers, and track subscription changes.

tasks.name: DB migration: create subscription_overrides table (user_id, tier, reason, admin_id, starts_at, expires_at, created_at)
DB migration: RLS policy for subscription_overrides (only service_role can write)
Create Edge Function: admin-override-subscription (sets tier override, logs to audit)
Create Edge Function: admin-revoke-override (removes tier override, reverts to RevenueCat tier)
Build Subscriptions overview page (table of all users with tier, source, expiry)
Build Subscription Detail section on User Detail page (RevenueCat data + any overrides)
Build Issue Free Upgrade modal (select tier, set duration or indefinite, add reason)
Build Revoke Override button with confirmation
Build Active Overrides list page (all users with admin-granted subscriptions)
Update mobile app useMembership hook to check subscription_overrides table first
Add subscription change history to User Detail page
Create useAdminSubscriptions hook (list, override, revoke)
Add audit log entries for all subscription actions

gateTests.name: Issue free Premium upgrade → user sees Premium features in mobile app
Issue free Family Premium upgrade → user sees Family features in mobile app
Free upgrade with expiry → reverts to Free tier after expiry date
Free upgrade indefinite → persists until manually revoked
Revoke override → user reverts to their RevenueCat subscription tier
Override takes priority over RevenueCat subscription
User with active RevenueCat sub + override → override wins
Active Overrides page shows all current overrides with expiry dates
All subscription actions logged in audit trail
E2E: issue free upgrade → verify in mobile app → revoke → verify reverted
Deprecation review: RevenueCat webhooks, Supabase cron for expiry checks

phases.name: Analytics Dashboard
phases.description: Real-time stats dashboard: MAU, DAU, subscription breakdowns, growth trends, and key engagement metrics.

tasks.name: DB migration: create analytics_daily table (date, dau, new_signups, new_connections, messages_sent, broadcasts_sent)
Create DB function: calculate_daily_analytics (runs daily via cron, populates analytics_daily)
Create DB function: get_current_stats (real-time MAU, DAU, total users, active subscriptions)
Create DB function: get_subscription_breakdown (count per tier: Free, Premium, Family Premium)
Install chart library (Recharts or Chart.js)
Build Analytics Dashboard page layout (stat cards + charts grid)
Build stat cards: Total Users, DAU, MAU, Active Subscriptions
Build Subscription Breakdown pie/donut chart (Free vs Premium vs Family Premium)
Build DAU/MAU trend line chart (last 30 days)
Build New Signups bar chart (last 30 days)
Build engagement metrics: avg connections per user, avg messages per day
Add date range selector for all charts
Create useAnalytics hook (current stats, trends, breakdowns)
Set up Supabase cron job to run calculate_daily_analytics nightly
Make Analytics Dashboard the default home page for admin portal

gateTests.name: DAU count matches users with activity in last 24 hours
MAU count matches users with activity in last 30 days
Subscription breakdown totals match total user count
Subscription breakdown correctly categorizes Free, Premium, Family Premium (including overrides)
DAU/MAU trend chart renders with correct data points
New signups chart matches actual registration dates
Date range selector updates all charts
Cron job runs and populates analytics_daily correctly
Stats cards show live data, not stale cache
Dashboard loads within 3 seconds
E2E: verify stats match known test data
Deprecation review: chart library version, Supabase cron/pg_cron setup

phases.name: Support Ticket System
phases.description: Receive, review, assign, and resolve support requests submitted from the mobile app. Email notifications on status changes.

tasks.name: DB migration: create support_tickets table (id, user_id, subject, description, category, status, priority, assigned_admin_id, created_at, updated_at, resolved_at)
DB migration: create support_ticket_messages table (id, ticket_id, sender_type [user|admin], message, created_at)
DB migration: RLS policies for support tables (admins can read/write all, users can read/write own)
Create Edge Function: support-ticket-notification (email user on status change or admin reply)
Update mobile app Help & Support form to create support_tickets row instead of sending email
Build Support Tickets list page (filterable by status: Open, In Progress, Resolved, Closed)
Add priority indicators (Low, Medium, High, Urgent) with color coding
Add category filter (Bug Report, Account Issue, Billing, Feature Request, General)
Build Ticket Detail page with full conversation thread
Build admin reply form on Ticket Detail page
Build status change controls (Open → In Progress → Resolved → Closed)
Build priority change controls
Build assign-to-admin dropdown
Add ticket count badges in sidebar nav (open tickets count)
Build My Tickets view (tickets assigned to current admin)
Create useAdminTickets hook (list, filter, update status, reply)
Add link from User Detail page to user's support tickets
Add audit log entries for ticket actions

gateTests.name: Mobile app support form → creates ticket row in support_tickets
Tickets list shows all tickets with correct status indicators
Filter by status works (Open, In Progress, Resolved, Closed)
Filter by category works
Filter by priority works
Ticket detail shows full conversation thread in chronological order
Admin reply → message appears in thread, user notified via email
Status change → user notified via email
Assign to admin → ticket appears in that admin's My Tickets view
Sidebar badge shows correct count of open tickets
User Detail page links to that user's tickets
All ticket actions logged in audit trail
E2E: user submits ticket → admin sees it → replies → user gets email → admin resolves → status updated
Deprecation review: Resend email API, Supabase Realtime for ticket updates

phases.name: Admin-to-User Messaging
phases.description: Send direct messages from admin portal that appear in users' in-app message conversations. Support for individual and broadcast messages.

tasks.name: DB migration: create system_user profile for admin messages (special user_id for 'RV Neighbors Team')
Create Edge Function: admin-send-message (creates conversation if needed, sends message as system user, triggers push)
Create Edge Function: admin-broadcast-message (sends to all users or filtered group, creates individual conversations)
Build Send Message modal on User Detail page (compose message to individual user)
Build Broadcast Message page (compose + recipient targeting: all users, by tier, by region)
Build recipient preview (show count and sample of who will receive broadcast)
Build sent messages history page (all admin-sent messages with delivery status)
Update mobile app Chat screen to show system user messages with special styling (admin badge)
Add push notification for admin messages
Create useAdminMessaging hook (send individual, send broadcast, history)
Add confirmation step before sending broadcast messages
Add audit log entries for all sent messages

gateTests.name: Send message to user → appears in their Messages tab in mobile app
Admin message shows 'RV Neighbors Team' as sender with admin badge
User receives push notification for admin message
Broadcast to all users → each user gets individual conversation
Broadcast to tier filter → only matching users receive message
Recipient preview shows accurate count before sending
Sent messages history shows all admin messages with timestamps
Confirmation modal prevents accidental broadcasts
All messages logged in audit trail
User can reply to admin message → admin sees reply (future consideration)
E2E: send individual message → verify in mobile app → send broadcast → verify all recipients
Deprecation review: Supabase insert patterns, push notification Edge Function

phases.name: Feature Request Tracker
phases.description: Track, categorize, prioritize, and manage feature requests from users and internal sources. Voting and status tracking.

tasks.name: DB migration: create feature_requests table (id, title, description, category, status, priority, submitted_by, vote_count, created_at, updated_at)
DB migration: create feature_request_votes table (request_id, user_id, created_at) with unique constraint
DB migration: create feature_request_comments table (id, request_id, admin_id, comment, created_at)
DB migration: RLS policies for feature request tables
Build Feature Requests list page (sortable by votes, date, status, priority)
Add status filter (New, Under Review, Planned, In Progress, Shipped, Declined)
Add category filter (Messaging, Maps, Connections, Subscriptions, UI/UX, Other)
Build Feature Request Detail page (description, votes, comments, status history)
Build Add Feature Request form (admin can submit requests from user feedback)
Build status change controls with comment requirement on status changes
Build admin comments section on Feature Request Detail page
Build priority assignment controls
Build Feature Request summary/stats (total, by status, most voted)
Auto-create feature request from support tickets categorized as 'Feature Request'
Create useFeatureRequests hook (list, create, update, comment)
Add audit log entries for feature request status changes

gateTests.name: Feature requests list loads with correct data
Sort by vote count shows most requested first
Filter by status works for all status values
Filter by category works
Create new feature request → appears in list
Change status → comment required, status history updated
Admin comment → appears in detail page thread
Support ticket with 'Feature Request' category → auto-creates feature request
Priority assignment persists and displays correctly
Summary stats match actual data
E2E: create request → add comments → change status → verify in list
Deprecation review: any new dependencies added in this phase

phases.name: Polish, Security & Production Prep
phases.description: Security audit, responsive design, error handling, performance optimization, and deployment hardening.

tasks.name: Security audit: verify all routes require admin authentication
Security audit: verify service_role key is never exposed to client bundle
Security audit: verify all Edge Functions validate admin JWT before executing
Security audit: add rate limiting to all admin Edge Functions
Security audit: input sanitization on all forms and Edge Functions
Add error boundaries and fallback UI for all pages
Add loading skeletons for all data-fetching pages
Add empty states for all list pages
Add toast notifications for success/error on all actions
Responsive design pass (desktop-first, but usable on tablet)
Dark mode support (optional but nice-to-have)
Performance: lazy load routes, optimize bundle size
Add keyboard shortcuts for common actions (Escape to close modals, etc.)
Final .env audit — no secrets in source code or client bundle
Remove all console.log statements
Configure production Vercel deployment with proper environment variables
Write admin user setup documentation (how to add new admins)

gateTests.name: All routes return 401/redirect when not authenticated
Service role key not present in client JavaScript bundle
All Edge Functions reject requests without valid admin JWT
Rate limiting blocks rapid repeated requests
XSS: script tags in user input are sanitized
Error boundaries catch render errors, show fallback
All pages show loading state while fetching
All list pages show empty state when no data
Toast notifications appear for all CRUD actions
Layout is usable at 1024px width (tablet)
No console.log in production build
Production deployment loads correctly on Vercel
Full E2E: login → dashboard → browse users → manage subscription → handle ticket → send message → review feature requests → logout
Deprecation review: all dependencies up to date, no deprecation warnings in build output
